clinical.chief_complaints: Burns to face, bilateral upper limbs, and chest following LPG cylinder explosion at home, 3 hours ago, severe pain
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Patient was cooking at home when LPG cylinder exploded. Sustained thermal burns to face, bilateral upper limbs, and anterior chest. Burns occurred approximately 3 hours ago. Patient brought to ER by neighbours. MLC has been registered at local police station.
clinical.provisional_diagnosis: Thermal burns — mixed 2nd and 3rd degree, 25% TBSA (face 4.5%, right upper limb 9%, left upper limb 9%, anterior chest 2.5%)
clinical.planned_procedure: Wound debridement, silver sulphadiazine dressings, planned split skin graft after wound preparation

examination_findings.general: Patient in severe pain, anxious, voice hoarse (possible inhalation injury), no frank stridor
examination_findings.cvs: Tachycardia, BP borderline, no murmur, peripheral pulses present
examination_findings.rs: Hoarse voice, no stridor, SpO2 94% on oxygen mask. Auscultation clear.
examination_findings.local: Burns extent mapped using Rule of 9: Face 4.5% (2nd degree, blistering), Right upper limb 9% (mixed 2nd-3rd degree, eschar forming), Left upper limb 9% (2nd degree, blistering), Anterior chest 2.5% (2nd degree). Total TBSA 25%. Burns appear mixed depth.

investigations.result_summary: CBC: Hb 15.2 g/dL, WBC 14800/cumm (stress response), Platelets 2.9 lakh. RFT: Creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, urea 28 mg/dL. Electrolytes: Na 138 mEq/L, K 4.1 mEq/L. ABG: pH 7.36, PaO2 88 on mask

non_clinical_documents_in_hand.label: MLC copy with FIR number (accidental burns)

treating_doctor.name: Dr. Karthik Rajan
treating_doctor.qualification: MS Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery, Burns Fellowship

clinical.notes: MLC case — accidental burns from LPG explosion. Clinical photo with Rule of 9 chart is mandatory for BM packages but NOT yet in hand — should appear as missing in document gap analysis. Follow-up photos at days 5, 10, 15, 20 will be required at claim stage. Possible inhalation injury needs assessment.
